metadata.title: Important Days and Events Current Affairs MCQs

questions.question: National Cancer Awareness Day is observed every year on which day?
questions.explanation: National Cancer Awareness Day is observed every year on 7th November in India since 2014. The day aims to spread awareness about the early detection, prevention, and treatment of cancer. Healthcare organisations, government bodies, and NGOs conduct awareness drives, seminars, and free screenings nationwide. India has an estimated 20–25 lakh active cancer patients, with about 7 lakh new cases reported each year. Nearly two-thirds of new cases are detected at an incurable stage.

questions.question: World Immunization Day is celebrated on which day every year?
questions.explanation: World Immunization Day is observed every year on November 10 to raise awareness about the importance of vaccines in preventing diseases and saving lives. Vaccines act as a barrier against infections and are one of the most effective public health measures in history. The day was established by the World Health Organization (WHO) in 2012. It marks the launch of the Expanded Programme on Immunization (EPI) in 1974, which aimed to provide vaccines to people worldwide.

questions.question: What is the theme of World Toilet Day 2025?
questions.explanation: World Toilet Day is observed every year on 19 November to raise awareness about the global sanitation crisis. It highlights the importance of safe and accessible toilets for public health, dignity, and environmental protection. The day was inspired by Singaporean philanthropist Jack Sim, who founded the NGO World Toilet Organisation (WTO) on 19 November. The United Nations (UN) recognised water and sanitation as human rights in 2010 and officially declared World Toilet Day on 14 July 2013. The theme for World Toilet Day 2025 is ‘Sanitation in a Changing World,’ focusing on future-ready sanitation systems.

questions.question: What is the theme of World AMR (Antimicrobial Resistance) Awareness Week (WAAW) 2025?

options.label: B
options.text: Act Now: Protect Our Present, Secure Our Future

answer.label: B
answer.text: Act Now: Protect Our Present, Secure Our Future

questions.explanation: World AMR Awareness Week (World Antimicrobial Resistance Awareness Week) is observed every year from 18–24 November to raise awareness about antimicrobial resistance. The week highlights that misuse and overuse of antimicrobials make microbes resistant, causing drug-resistant infections that are harder to treat. It promotes the One Health approach, which links human, animal, and environmental health to tackle resistance together. It is a global initiative led by the World Health Organization (WHO). The 2025 theme is “Act Now: Protect Our Present, Secure Our Future,” urging immediate action to safeguard medicines for today and future generations.

questions.question: Where was the Chanakya Defence Dialogue 2025 hosted by the Indian Army?
questions.explanation: Chanakya Defence Dialogue 2025 began at the Manekshaw Centre in New Delhi. It is organised by the Indian Army with the Centre for Land Warfare Studies. The focus is on Sashakt, Surakshit and Viksit Bharat. Key themes include cyber, space, artificial intelligence, and cognitive warfare. The Army’s transformation roadmap includes HOP 2032, STEP 2037 and JUMP 2047. Defence self-reliance and indigenisation were stressed as national priorities. Three sessions covered strategic victory, defence reforms and civil–military fusion.

questions.question: Mahaparinirvan Diwas is observed every year on which day?
questions.explanation: Mahaparinirvan Diwas is observed every year on 6 December to mark the death anniversary of Dr Bhimrao Ramji Ambedkar, the chief architect of the Indian Constitution. Dr Ambedkar passed away on 6 December 1956 and is remembered for fighting caste discrimination and promoting social justice. Thousands gathered at Chaityabhoomi in Mumbai to pay tributes, with flower petals showered and copies of the Constitution distributed. President Droupadi Murmu, Prime Minister Narendra Modi, and other leaders offered floral tributes. The day inspires people to follow his ideals of equality, justice, and democracy.

questions.question: International Day of Commemoration and Dignity of the Victims of the Crime of Genocide is observed every year on which day?
questions.explanation: The United Nations’ International Day of Commemoration and Dignity of the Victims of the Crime of Genocide and of the Prevention of this Crime is observed every year on 9 December to honour all victims of genocide worldwide. The day highlights the responsibility of individual states and the global community to prevent genocide, punish perpetrators, and uphold human rights and dignity.

questions.question: Where was the 3rd Session of the Council of the International Organization for Marine Aids to Navigation (IALA) inaugurated?
questions.explanation: The 3rd Session of the Council of the International Organization for Marine Aids to Navigation (IALA) was recently inaugurated virtually in Mumbai by the Union Minister for Ports, Shipping and Waterways. Its motto is “Successful Voyages, Sustainable Planet.” It aims to improve and standardise aids to navigation worldwide, promote safe and efficient vessel movement, support new technological developments and strengthen international cooperation.

questions.question: Minorities Rights Day in India is observed annually on which day?
questions.explanation: Minorities Rights Day in India is observed annually on 18 December. The day aims to preserve, protect, and promote the rights of minority communities while emphasizing equality, safety, and mutual respect. It encourages conversations about inclusion and fair treatment in daily life. India’s minority communities are an important part of its diverse social, cultural, linguistic, and religious fabric. It serves as a reminder that equal rights are essential for a healthy democracy. The day spreads awareness about constitutional rights and protections available to minority groups.

questions.question: National Mathematics Day is observed annually on which day?
questions.explanation: National Mathematics Day is observed annually on December 22 to honor the birth anniversary of Srinivasa Ramanujan, one of India’s most influential mathematicians. The day was formally instituted by the Government of India in December 2011, with 2012 declared as National Mathematics Year to promote mathematical learning and research nationwide. December 22 was chosen to recognize Ramanujan’s extraordinary contributions, including solutions to problems considered intractable in his lifetime and their lasting global impact. The observance encourages students, researchers, and academic institutions to engage with mathematics as central to innovation, technology, and scientific progress.
